title: Dust-amount payment not rejected at /pay entry
description: Zero zatoshi is now caught at /pay entry time. But sub-dust amounts (1–999 zatoshi, below DUST_THRESHOLD=1000) are not. A user typing '/pay bob 0.000001' (100 zatoshi) broadcasts a PaymentAction::Request to all room members, receives an address back, then gets a TxBuildError::DustAmount rejection when send_payment() fires. The user has already leaked payment intent to the room for a payment that could have been rejected locally. Fix: read DUST_THRESHOLD from tx_builder at /pay entry and reject with a clear message ('amount must be at least X zatoshi').
close_reason: Fixed: DUST_THRESHOLD made pub in tx_builder.rs, imported in commands.rs. /pay handler now checks amount_zatoshi < DUST_THRESHOLD and prints 'amount must be at least 1000 zatoshi (0.00001000 ZEC)' before creating a session. Replaces the zero-only check.
